---
title: "FW 536 — Day 2 afternoon lab (v3 · aquatic & fisheries) — Your Name"
author: "Your Name Here"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    self_contained: true
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: show
    theme: cosmo
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

# Day 2 afternoon lab — Linear models problem set

**Problem set version:** **v3 — aquatic & fisheries theme**.
(The companion version is [v2 — forest & songbird](problem_set_v2.html); you choose whichever theme fits your interests.)

This template matches [`problem_set_v3.html`](problem_set_v3.html). Read each problem statement there, write your work below, **Knit** to HTML, and upload the `.html` to the matching Canvas assignment.

For setup help see the [R Markdown tutorial](../rmarkdown_tutorial.html).

---

# Setup

```{r}
# Load packages you'll need. Add or remove as appropriate.
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(MASS)        # for glm.nb
library(effects)
library(emmeans)
```

---

# Problem 6 — Stream discharge × season interaction on macroinvertebrate counts

> Macroinvertebrate counts per Surber sample from 90 collections; predictors are discharge (m³/s, continuous) and season (factor: spring reference, fall). Poisson fit:

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 7 — CPUE for a longline fishery — variable hook numbers

> Across 200 longline sets, the number of hooks varies from 500 to 5000. You record bycatch counts of yelloweye rockfish per set. Two analyses:

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 8 — Sockeye redd success vs. substrate gravel size

> Logistic regression of redd-survival to emergence on median gravel diameter (mm). Intercept $-1.5$, slope $+0.06$ per mm.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 9 — Eelgrass biomass vs sediment grain size

> A Gamma GLM with log link returns, for the contrast "fine sediment vs medium sediment", $\hat\beta = -0.65$ with 95% CI $(-0.95, -0.35)$ on the log scale.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 10 — Estuarine green sturgeon occupancy

> Occupancy (1/0) at 250 estuarine sites modeled as glm(present ~ salinity + depth, family = binomial). Intercept $-2.5$; salinity slope $+0.10$ per ppt; depth slope $+0.20$ per m.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# (Optional) Embed a photo of hand-written work

For problems easier to do on paper, photograph and embed:

```{r, echo=FALSE, eval=FALSE, out.width="60%", fig.cap="Hand-derivation for Problem N"}
knitr::include_graphics("images/problemN_derivation.jpg")
```

(Change `eval=FALSE` to `eval=TRUE` after saving an image. See the [R Markdown tutorial](../rmarkdown_tutorial.html) for details.)

---

# Submission checklist

- [ ] Answered every afternoon problem from `problem_set_v3.html`.
- [ ] Knitted to HTML without errors.
- [ ] Photos (if any) embedded with `self_contained: true` in the YAML.
- [ ] Uploaded the `.html` to the matching Canvas assignment.
